content:
Case 8:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 78
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Progressive fatigue, dyspnea on exertion, reduced appetite, and high fever with productive cough
- Vital signs: BP 86/54 mmHg, HR 128/min regular, T° 39.2 °C, RR 28/min, O₂ saturation 89% on room air.
Setting: Emergency Department / Acute Care (Initial Septic Presentation)
Capabilities: You are assessing an acutely ill patient with sepsis in an acute-care environment. You can perform urgent clinical evaluation, obtain baseline investigations, and arrange emergent non-invasive imaging appropriate for the acute situation. Invasive procedures and complex diagnostic evaluations usually follow once the patient is at least partially stabilized.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination"}}

History Physical:
14 Feb 2025: The patient presented to the emergency department with progressive fatigue, dyspnea on exertion, and reduced appetite. He reported high fever over the past 24 hours, associated with chills and productive cough. On examination: Hypotension (BP 86/54 mmHg), tachycardia (HR 128/min), tachypnea (RR 28/min), and fever (T° 39.2 °C). O₂ saturation 89 % on room air, improving to 95 % with 5 L/min nasal cannula. Mucous membranes were dry. No focal neurological deficits were identified. Current medication: amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-0, atorvastatin 20 mg 0-0-1, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20 000 IU weekly, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, co-trimoxazole 960 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), levodopa/carbidopa 200/50 mg 1-1-1, posaconazole 300 mg 1-0-0, levofloxacin 500 mg 1-0-0 (standby medication, started on 13 Feb 2025).
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count"}}

Cbc:
CBC: Hb 7.8 g/dL, WBC 1.1 ×10⁹/L, ANC 0.3 ×10⁹/L, Plt 18 ×10⁹/L; MCV normal, MCH normal; differential: 22% neutrophils, 10% monocytes, 60% lymphocytes, 8% blasts.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "chest X-ray"}}

'chest X-ray' not available. Try 'ct_chest' instead.
This case does not include a chest X‑ray report. A related imaging item available in this round is CT of the chest; you can request 'ct_chest' for cross‑sectional chest imaging findings.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "ct_chest"}}

Ct Chest:
14 Feb 2025: CT scan: discrete bilateral patchy ground-glass opacities and small consolidations in the lower lobes. No pleural effusion.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "comprehensive metabolic panel and lactate"}}

Chemistry Inflammation:
Na 129 mmol/L, K 4.6 mmol/L, creatinine 2.1 mg/dL, BUN 42 mg/dL, AST 98 U/L, ALT 76 U/L, bilirubin 2.1 mg/dL, LDH 435 U/L, uric acid 7.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.42 mmol/L, phosphate 3.9 mg/dL, lactate 4.5 mmol/L, CK 72 U/L, CRP 25.7 mg/dL, PCT 3.5 ng/mL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "peripheral blood smear morphology"}}

'cbc' already provided.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "blood cultures"}}

'extended_diagnostics' available in round 2.
Blood cultures fall under microbiology in the extended diagnostics set, which is not available until a later round. Consider requesting items available in this round such as CBC, chemistry/inflammatory labs, coagulation, or imaging (CT chest or abdominal ultrasound).
(3 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "coagulation profile"}}

Coagulation:
Coagulation: aPTT 39 sec, INR 1.8.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with pancytopenia presenting with neutropenic sepsis and septic shock", "differentials": ["MDS with transformation to AML", "Severe sepsis with bone marrow suppression (leukemoid reaction)", "Aplastic anemia with superimposed infection"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Aggressive fluid resuscitation for septic shock with vasopressor support (norepinephrine) if MAP <65 mmHg after fluids", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics: upgrade to antipseudomonal beta-lactam (piperacillin-tazobactam or cefepime) plus additional Gram-positive coverage (vancomycin) given severe neutropenia and hemodynamic instability", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Transfuse platelets (target >10-20 \u00d710\u2079/L, higher if active bleeding) and packed red blood cells (target Hb >8 g/dL)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate filgrastim (G-CSF) 5 mcg/kg daily for neutropenia recovery", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Urgent bone marrow biopsy and aspirate with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular studies when hemodynamically stabilized", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "ICU admission for hemodynamic monitoring and management of septic shock", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Monitor for tumor lysis syndrome given high LDH and uric acid; initiate allopurinol and ensure adequate hydration", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Correction of coagulopathy with vitamin K or FFP if bleeding develops", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with pancytopenia presenting with neutropenic sepsis and septic shock
differentials: Severe sepsis with bone marrow suppression (leukemoid reaction)

treatment_plan.action: Aggressive fluid resuscitation for septic shock with vasopressor support (norepinephrine) if MAP <65 mmHg after fluids

Empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics: upgrade to antipseudomonal beta-lactam (piperacillin-tazobactam or cefepime) plus additional Gram-positive coverage (vancomycin) given severe neutropenia and hemodynamic instability
Transfuse platelets (target >10-20 ×10⁹/L, higher if active bleeding) and packed red blood cells (target Hb >8 g/dL)
Initiate filgrastim (G-CSF) 5 mcg/kg daily for neutropenia recovery
Urgent bone marrow biopsy and aspirate with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular studies when hemodynamically stabilized
ICU admission for hemodynamic monitoring and management of septic shock
Monitor for tumor lysis syndrome given high LDH and uric acid; initiate allopurinol and ensure adequate hydration
Correction of coagulopathy with vitamin K or FFP if bleeding develops

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow biopsy and aspirate with flow cytometry and cytogenetics"}}

response.request: bone marrow biopsy and aspirate with flow cytometry and cytogenetics

content:
Bone Marrow:
Smears are of limited quality with low overall cellularity and very few spicules. Scattered blasts are present, accounting for approximately 10–15 % of nucleated cells. The blasts are medium to large in size with a high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, round to irregular nuclei, and finely dispersed chromatin. The cytoplasm is basophilic, occasionally containing azurophilic granules and rare vacuoles.
Dysplastic changes are observed in the residual myeloid series with hypogranulation, while erythropoiesis is markedly reduced, showing occasional megaloblastoid forms. Megakaryopoiesis is sparse, with small hypolobated megakaryocytes.
Flow cytometry demonstrated 23 % blasts expressing CD34⁺, CD117⁺, HLA-DR⁺, CD13⁺, CD33⁺, and CD38 (dim), with dim CD123 and weak MPO expression. There was no aberrant co-expression of CD7 or CD56
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "cytogenetics and molecular studies including FISH for common AML abnormalities and mutation screening for NPM1, FLT3-ITD/TKD, and CEBPA"}}

Ultrasound Abdomen Echo:
Abdominal ultrasound: Normal sonographic appearance of liver, spleen, kidneys, and pancreas. No hydronephrosis. Collapsing inferior vena cava with respiration. No lymphadenopathy. Reduced left-ventricular systolic function (LVEF 43 %) with global hypokinesia and mild tachycardia during examination, no significant valvular stenosis, and a small pericardial effusion (< 0.5 cm).

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "treatment_history including transplant details and prior chemotherapy regimens"}}

response.request: treatment_history including transplant details and prior chemotherapy regimens

content:
Treatment History:
07/25/2018: Diagnosis of AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC, WHO 2016). 08/03/2018: Induction I with CPX-351: Liposomal daunorubicin 44 mg/m² (≈ 90 mg/day) + cytarabine 100 mg/m² (≈ 205 mg/day) on days 1, 3, and 5. Total dose per course: daunorubicin ≈ 270 mg, cytarabine ≈ 615 mg. Result: hypocellular bone marrow without detectable residual AML (MLFS)
10/21/2018 – Induction II with CPX-351: Same dosing as Induction I (days 1 and 3: daunorubicin ≈ 90 mg/day, cytarabine ≈ 205 mg/day). Result: CR with incomplete count recovery. 01/09/2019 – Allogeneic stem-cell transplantation (alloSCT): From a female HLA-identical unrelated donor after conditioning with fludarabine 30 mg/m² ≈ 61 mg/day i.v. (days -6 to -2), treosulfan 10 g/m² = 20.5 g/day i.v. (days –6 to –4), and rabbit-ATG 2.5 mg/kg (days -3 to 1). Donor/recipient CMV: negative/positive; both A Rh positive. Immunosuppression: cyclosporine 2.5 mg/kg BID (225 mg BID) starting day -3 and mycophenolate 1 g BID from day -1. 01/28/2019 (day +19): Bone marrow: complete molecular remission, donor chimerism 99.94%. 03/25/2020: EBV reactivation (152 copies/10⁵ cells), resolved spontaneously. 04/20/2020: Day +100 marrow: cytogenetic CR, donor chimerism 99.81%. 05/16/2020: Chimerism declined to 99.57% → DLI1 (1x10⁶ CD3⁺/kg) on 06/07/2020; DLI3 (5 x10⁶ CD3⁺/kg) on 07/07/2020; DLI3 (1x10⁷ CD3⁺/kg) on 08/10/2020 → donor chimerism 99.89%. 09/25/2020: DLI4 (5x10⁷ CD3⁺/kg). In October 2020, the patient developed acute gastrointestinal GvHD grade III. GvHD was treated with prednisolone 2 mg/kg (175 mg/day), followed by ruxolitinib 10 mg BID, etanercept 25 mg BIW x 4 doses, and vedolizumab 300 mg q14d → partial clinical resolution by 11/04/2020. 11/2020 – 08/2021: Serial marrows showed sustained CR, donor chimerism > 99.9%. 01/14/2023: Hematologic relapse: 14 % blasts by morphology, 27 % by FACS; mutations in ASXL1 (VAF 8 %), STAG2 (VAF 8 %), RAD21 (VAF 9 %), SRSF2 (VAF 8 %).
Chimerism: donor 98.6 %, recipient 1.4 %. 01/23/2023 – Start of azacitidine/venetoclax: Azacitidine 75 mg/m² s.c. days 1-7 (total = 1100 mg). Venetoclax ramp-up with posaconazole co-medication: 10 mg (d1), 20 mg (d2), 50 mg (d3), then 70 mg/day from d4 onward. 02/22/2023: Bone marrow CR (4 % blasts by morphology, 1 % by FACS), full molecular clearance of mutations.
CMV/EBV PCR negative. Continued azacitidine/venetoclax for 6 cycles, intervals gradually extended to 5 weeks, venetoclax duration shortened to 14 and then to 10 days per cycle due to cytopenia. 08/26/2023: Hospitalized for worsening Parkinsonian symptoms (bradykinesia, rigidity, tremor). No acute cause identified. Patient chose to stop azacitidine/venetoclax thereafter; neurological symptoms persisted. 01/12/2024: Bone marrow puncture: ongoing CR, donor chimerism 99.89 % (recipient 0.11 %).
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "next-generation sequencing and molecular profiling of current bone marrow aspirate"}}

response.request: next-generation sequencing and molecular profiling of current bone marrow aspirate

content:
Molecular Panel:
Mutations detected in ASXL1 (VAF 12%), DNMT3A (VAF 10%), SRSF2 (VAF 8%), STAG2 (VAF 7%), and RAD21 (VAF 6%). No pathogenic variants identified in FLT3-ITD/TKD, NPM1, CEBPA, IDH1, IDH2, TP53, RUNX1, NRAS, KRAS, TET2, EZH2, BCOR, BCORL1, U2AF1, ZRSR2, SF3B1, SETBP1, ETV6, CBL, KIT. No KMT2Ar.

monitor CMV/EBV PCR weekly", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Aggressive supportive care: transfusion support with irradiated, leukoreduced, CMV-negative blood products; consider iron chelation if transfusion-dependent", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Cardiology optimization of heart failure therapy given LVEF 43% and avoidance of cardiotoxic agents; aggressive volume management during chemotherapy", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Palliative care consultation for symptom management and advance care planning given poor-risk disease, age 78, and ECOG 2 status; establish goals of care", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consider donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) if patient achieves remission with salvage therapy to consolidate response, or as bridge if chemotherapy fails", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Relapsed acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC) following allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant, late relapse (6 years post-transplant) with mixed chimerism indicating recipient-derived relapse
differentials: Secondary AML from prior chemotherapy (anthracycline/cytarabine exposure)
Donor-derived leukemia (ruled out by XX/XY pattern showing recipient male cells)

treatment_plan.action: Re-induction with azacitidine 75 mg/m² days 1-7 plus venetoclax ramp-up to 70 mg daily days 1-21 (dose reduced due to posaconazole interaction and prior intolerance)
Enrollment in clinical trial evaluating novel agents for relapsed post-transplant AML if available (menin inhibitor, CD47 antibody, or FLT3-targeted therapy if mutation acquired)
Continuation of infectious prophylaxis: posaconazole for antifungal, valaciclovir for HSV/VZV, and PJP prophylaxis with continued co-trimoxazole; monitor CMV/EBV PCR weekly
Aggressive supportive care: transfusion support with irradiated, leukoreduced, CMV-negative blood products; consider iron chelation if transfusion-dependent
Cardiology optimization of heart failure therapy given LVEF 43% and avoidance of cardiotoxic agents; aggressive volume management during chemotherapy
Palliative care consultation for symptom management and advance care planning given poor-risk disease, age 78, and ECOG 2 status; establish goals of care
Consider donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) if patient achieves remission with salvage therapy to consolidate response, or as bridge if chemotherapy fails
